rows.description: Soluta Scholari Cogengensi
rows.notes: Top-of-page title is legible as 'Soluta' and 'Scholari' but the final word is scribal/abbreviated and somewhat unclear; I have transcribed it as 'Cogengensi' as best fit to the handwriting.

rows.description: Jany 17. Pd Wm Le Coutins share of Mr Symollys Tine
rows.notes: Date and description readable with minor letter uncertainties. Amount columns align clearly to the three ruling lines: 4 pounds, 6 shillings, 11 and a half pence (the pence shows a clear half-penny loop).

rows.description: June 20. Pd Ditto share of Southnewington Tine
rows.notes: Description 'Ditto ... Southnewington' slightly abbreviated but clear from context. Amounts align to columns: 3£, 12s, 9½d (half-penny symbol visible).

rows.description: Pd Ditto of William Evans of Little ???
rows.notes: Name/place at line end is partly faded/uncertain ('Little ...'); amounts are clearly aligned: 0£, 5s, 8½d (half-penny visible).

rows.description: Ditto of Sheppards assignment of his Lease
rows.notes: Description legible though script is cramped. Pence looks like '10' with a trailing loop that I interpret as '3q' (three farthings = 0.75). The loop is slightly ambiguous (could be a flourish) so confidence reduced.

rows.description: Jany 24. Mayor Upton pro Decremt ad Oct: Nat: 1766.
rows.notes: Date and name (Mayor Upton) are clear. Amount reads 9£, 15s, 1¼d — the pence has a small quarter-farthing style loop interpreted as 1/4 (0.25); the fractional symbol is unusual but visible.

rows.description: Soluta varia.
rows.notes: Clear centered section heading 'Soluta varia.' with no amounts.
Year written in margin; clear and acts as a section label.

rows.description: Febb. 10. Pd Do: Bill for Bottry Books
rows.notes: Date and 'Bill for ... Books' clear enough; amounts clearly '3 0 0' (three pounds exactly) aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence lines.

rows.description: - ditto for Kitchen Books
rows.notes: Continuation 'ditto' line; amounts clearly 1£ 7s 0d as aligned in the columns.

rows.description: - 11 - to Clemens Corn price
rows.notes: Date mark '11' and description 'to Clemens corn price' slightly cramped; amount reads 0£ 1s 0d — shilling digit clear, pence blank/zero.

rows.description: - 21. Allowed Mr Sadlers Proper Money
rows.notes: Description legible though surname may be 'Sadler'/similar; amount clearly 0£ 6s 0d in alignment.

rows.description: - 28. Pd Ch. Ell. Unit Rent due Mich: 1766.
rows.notes: Abbreviated names but amounts are crisply written: 0£, 18s, 0d.

rows.description: March 25. Pd Meyers Copys of last year
rows.notes: Description legible with minor spelling variants; amount 0£ 14s 0d clear in its column.

rows.description: - 28. placed to ye acct of Weston arrears due Michl: 1766.
rows.notes: Description is readable as an accounting placement to Weston; the pounds and shillings (6 and 17) are clear. The final pence digit is faint/ambiguous (looks like '4' in some strokes but better aligns as '3' to match the ledger total at bottom); I have recorded 3d but confidence is moderate.

rows.description: 31. Pd Merton 2 year Dividend due Ladyday 1766
rows.notes: Large-figure payment (90£) clearly written; shillings and pence (1s 10d) are legible though their size and ink thickness reduce absolute certainty slightly.

rows.description: Pd Ditto to Michl: 1766
rows.notes: Repeat/deliverance line mirroring the prior Merton dividend entry; amounts identical to the previous line and alignment is clear.

rows.description: Pd Mr Bishop of Islip for Journeys to Merton in 1766.
rows.notes: Name and purpose are legible; amount 1£ 11s 6d clearly aligned and the pence '6' is unambiguous.

rows.description: April 4. Lady day Corn Price
rows.notes: Short descriptive line; amount reads 0£ 1s 0d and is clearly placed under the columns.

rows.description: - J. Pd Cook Bill for Gardening &c for ye former year
rows.notes: Description and amounts legible; amount 19£ 11s 0d is crisply written and well-aligned.

rows.description: - 13. Tuerlis former Bill in full
rows.notes: Surname spelling slightly uncertain ('Tuerlis'/'Teurlis'), but amount 15£ 0s 0d is clearly written in the columns.

rows.description: - 28. Townsends former Bill in full
rows.notes: Entry text 'Townsends former Bill in full' is clear; amount 10£ 2s 6d is crisply formed and unambiguous.

rows.description: May 6. Pd Collins Chandler in part of his Bill
rows.notes: Name and purpose legible; amount clearly 20£ 0s 0d.

rows.description: Total
rows.notes: Final ruled total at bottom right of page reads '£: 277 15 4 1/2' clearly. Note: to reconcile the written page total with line-by-line readings I interpreted the March 28 pence as 3d (rather than a possible 4d) because the totals add to the printed page sum only with that reading; the March 28 pence digit is faint and ambiguous so I explicitly chose 3d to match the ledger total.
